select_when: The environment is unstable enough that simple linear prediction is giving false confidence.
Minor disturbances, feedback, and path dependence make precise long-range forecasting unreliable.
Decision quality depends more on robustness and adaptive monitoring than on exact prediction.
The system appears orderly at a distance but is highly sensitive underneath.
avoid_when: Teams are using chaos rhetoric to imply nothing can be understood or improved.
A bounded mechanical process with stable variables is being treated as chaotic when simpler diagnosis would suffice.

curation_notes.summary: Focused on nonlinear instability, limits of prediction, and the move toward robustness and monitoring.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader complexity-philosophy sprawl because the raw source is already strongest on prediction limits and adaptive design.
open_questions: The source overlaps with butterfly-effect and risk planning, but the operational file stays on chaos as a caution against false precision.

select_when.note: The raw source is explicit on instability, nonlinear effects, sensitive dependence, and robustness over precise prediction.

avoid_when.note: The source warns against fatalism and against applying chaos language where simpler mechanisms suffice.

input_type.note: The input label compresses the raw activation pattern around unstable nonlinear environments.

output_type.note: The output label keeps the intervention on robustness and monitoring rather than exact forecasting.

reasoning_types.note: The source supports system-level causal tracing under nonlinear instability.
